sections.paragraph:
ἀνασείω (Hes. et al.; PTebt 28, 20 [II b.c.]; Jos., Bell. 5, 120) (lit. ‘shake’, ‘brandish’, e.g. a shield Hes., Sc. 344; Syn. σείω q.v.) to cause to be disturbed, stir up, disturb, upset, incite (cp. our colloq. ‘shake up’) (Diod S 13, 91, 4; 14, 10, 3; 17, 62, 5; 18, 10, 1; Is 36:18 Aq., Sym.) w. acc. τ. ὄχλον Mk 15:11. τ. λαόν Lk 23:5 (cp. Dionys. Hal. 8, 81 τὸ πλῆθος; Philod., Rhet. II 290 Sudh.) τὴν Ἰκονείων πόλιν ἀνασείει, ἔτι δὲ καὶ τὴν σὴν Θέκλαν AcPl Ox 6, 20 (=Aa I 242, 1).—M-M. TW.

Hes Hes , date uncertain, perh. before VI b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

PTebt PTebt = The Tebtunis Papyri—List 4

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

lit. lit. = literal(ly); literature (refererences to [scholarly] literature)

e.g. e.g. = exempli gratia (for example)

Syn.

Syn. = KAland, Synopsis Quattuor Evangeliorum—List 6

syn. = synopsis, synoptic

q.v. q.v. = quod vide (which see, see that which has just been mentioned)

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

colloq. colloq. = colloquial

Diod S Diod S , I b.c.—List 5

Aq Aq , II a.d.—List 5

Sym. Sym. = Symmachus, Greek version of the OT—List 2, beg., 5

acc. acc. = accusative

Philod Philod , I b.c.—List 5

AcPl Ox 6 AcPl Ox 6 = Acts of Paul, POxy 6—List 1

Aa Aa = Acta apostolorum apocrypha—List 5

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 71
